sourceMovie.title: Extraction 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Extraction 2 (2023) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shoot 'Em Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shoot 'Em Up (2007) operates in the same action as Extraction 2, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: War (2007) belongs to the same conversation as Extraction 2 — both anchor their action in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Memory
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Extraction 2's action stayed with you, Memory (2022) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Substitute
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Extraction 2 excels in action, The Substitute (1996) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Road to Perdition
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Road to Perdition (2002) is the natural next step after Extraction 2: same thriller DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and crime elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Ballerina
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Extraction 2 who respond to its action will find Ballerina (2023) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Triple Threat
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Extraction 2 fans keep landing on Triple Threat (2019) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Fight or Flight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fight or Flight draws from the same action well as Extraction 2, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2025 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Weekend in Taipei
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Weekend in Taipei (2024) captures the action appeal of Extraction 2 without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts Weekend in Taipei at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Debt Collectors
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Extraction 2 — that funny edge, the crime — translates cleanly into Debt Collectors (2020). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Extraction 2?
faq.answer: Try Shoot 'Em Up, War, Memory—each shares DNA with Extraction 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Extraction 2?
faq.answer: Extraction 2 (2023) is best described as Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Extraction 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Extraction Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
